nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; often found on outer reef slopes with silty substrates, shallow waters to about 20 m depth. Juveniles have been observed drifting with surface weeds; adults tend to associate with Sargassum rafts seasonally.

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on benthic organisms including algae (spirulina, nori), small crustaceans (krill, mysis, brine shrimp), sponges, and zooplankton. In captivity, varied frozen preparations are generally recommended; vitamin and fatty acid supplements are often advised as this species can lose condition quickly. Multiple small feedings per day tend to work well.

avoidWith: aggressive or boisterous species that outcompete for food

summary: A shy, peaceful saltwater filefish reaching up to 25 cm, generally recommended for fish-only systems due to a tendency to nip at corals and eat small invertebrates. Best kept singly in a well-established tank with ample rockwork and swimming space.
careNotes:
The Honeycomb Filefish (Cantherhines pardalis) is generally considered a peaceful species but is not regarded as reef safe — it has been known to nip at soft and stony corals and will often consume shrimps, crabs, snails, and other small invertebrates. It is best suited to fish-only or fish-only-with-live-rock (FOWLR) aquariums.

This species tends to be rather shy and secretive, seeking refuge among rockwork, and generally benefits from a calm tank with minimal competition for food. It has been reported to acclimate poorly when disturbed, so patience during initial introduction is advisable.

Diet should be varied: frozen preparations including mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, squid, and spirulina-based foods are generally recommended. Vitamin and fatty acid supplements are often suggested given this species' reportedly higher metabolic demands; individuals can become thin without adequate nutrition.

Coloration varies considerably by collection location — specimens have been observed in mottled grey-brown, dark brown, and grey phases with networks of polygonal spots. A distinctive white spot is present at the rear base of the second dorsal fin.

Minimum tank size is a topic of some disagreement across sources: estimates range from approximately 900 L (ReefApp) to 1,000 L (AquaInfo/aquainfo.nl) for adults; 250+ gallon (~950 L) recommendations appear frequently in the hobby. A 1,000 L (approximately 265 gal) minimum is used here, biased toward the larger end per caretaker guidance that this species needs ample open swimming space despite being a slow swimmer.

Temperature: sources differ — FishBase reports a wild range of 24.7–29.3°C, while Aquatics Unlimited lists 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) for aquarium keeping. An averaged range of approximately 23–27°C is used here. Care level assessments also vary: LiveAquaria and AquaInfo rate this species as moderate (intermediate), while Aquatics Unlimited rates it advanced. Intermediate is used here as the majority assessment.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Collection page; provided temperament (peaceful), reef compatibility (no), and care level (moderate). Full species detail page not accessible; general filefish guidance also extracted.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Primary cross-check. Confirmed scientific classification (Monacanthidae, Tetraodontiformes), max size 25 cm TL (common 15 cm), wild temperature range 24.7–29.3°C, native range (Eastern Atlantic, Indo-West Pacific, Red Sea, to southern Japan), depth 0–20 m, solitary behavior, and reef-associated habitat.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Provided minimum tank size 900 L, salinity SG ~1.026, adult size up to 25 cm, diet (omnivore; algae, small crustaceans, sponges, zooplankton), not definitively reef safe.

additional.name: AquaInfo
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Provided minimum tank size 1,000 L, pH 8.1–8.4, care level moderate, temperament peaceful/shy, not reef safe (nips corals and eats larger invertebrates).

additional.name: Aquatics Unlimited
additional.notes: Additional cross-check. Provided temperature 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.020–1.025, KH 8–12, adult size ~9.5 in (~24.1 cm), care level advanced, temperament peaceful, not reef safe. Temperature range is lower than FishBase wild data; averaged for final values.
